free_text: The (g,p) reaction at E-gamma about 60 MeV.

microtron followed by the stretcher ring.
The magnetically tagged photons.
Two telescopes each consisting of
two silicon strip detectors (SSDS) and one hyperpure
germanium detector (HPGE).
Energy spectra corresponding to several angular
bins were generated, and the yield for a specific
transition obtained by integration the number of
counts in a discrete peak.
Angular distributions for the 12C, 27Al(g,p)
reactions.
Reaction for data absolute normalization.
A study of the (g,p) reaction on 12C, 14N, 27Al,
and 51V targets, at photon energies Eg about 60 MeV,
was reported. Angular distributions in the range
theta-p = 60 - 120 degree were presented for
transitions leading to the ground state and several
low-lying states in each residual nucleus. The
relative population of the residual nucleus states
is similar to those found in (e,e'p) and (d,3He)
experiments, apart from the well-known exception of
the peak at 6.8 MeV in the 12C(g,p) reaction.
Direct-knockout calculations fail to account for the
magnitude of the (g,p) results, which indicates the
need for calculations that include effects such as
meson exchange or short-range correlations. Strength
seen at higher excitation energies is attributed to
inclusive (g,p) events. The excitation-energy spectra
have been compared with quasi-deuteron model
calculations which assume a (g,np) reaction where the
neutron goes undetected. The shape of 51V excitation
spectrum is compared to a pre-equilibrium model
calculation. The results appear to indicate that
two-nucleon absorption of the photon is important.
Data from the tables 1 - 3.
Data were compiled at the USA BNL NNDC by
V.V.Varlamov.
